final_answer:
Yes, I implemented the plan in `x/stake` and the checks are passing.

Why this matters: validator admission is now deterministic and single-path (no side-tx join mutation path), signer authorization is enforced in `ValidatorJoin`, and the proto registration panics that were blocking stake tests are fixed so this path is build/test safe.

What I changed:
1. Made validator join MsgServer-only and removed join side/post routing in [side_msg_server.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/side_msg_server.go).
2. Enforced sender/signer parity and kept state mutation in `ValidatorJoin` in [msg_server.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/msg_server.go).
3. Replaced panic-prone msgservice descriptor registration with explicit response registration in [codec.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/codec.go).
4. Fixed generated proto service/type-name drift by adding alias registrations and aligning service method paths with the embedded descriptor in [tx.pb.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/tx.pb.go) and [query.pb.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/query.pb.go).
5. Updated join side-handler tests to assert join side/post handlers are not registered in [side_msg_server_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/side_msg_server_test.go).

Checks run:
- `go test ./x/stake/...` ✅
- `go test ./app -run '^$'` ✅
- `go test ./bridge/processor -run '^$'` ✅
- `go test ./cmd/giltconsd -run '^$'` ✅

Next concrete step:
1. Regenerate stake protobuf outputs from source (`make proto-gen`) so generated files are fully canonical and you can drop manual alias/service-name drift fixes cleanly.
